Module 2:
The Mental Triggers
!
Executive Summary
Mental triggers are built into our brains. Evolutionary psychology shows
why these triggers exists, and we’re going to discuss them in this module.
!
Triggers
• 	Stories
• 	We’ve been trained for thousands of years to listen to stories
• 	Event Based
• 	People love events and enjoy feeling like they’re part of something
bigger than the themselves (significance)
• 	Having people go through an event TOGETHER is the core piece of
ritual
• 	Community
• 	Act like how you want people in your community to act
• 	Virtual communities are very strong
!
1	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 1 of 5 --

• 	Social Proof
• 	We all look to other people for clues on how we should act
• 	Ex: you’ll go to the restaurant with more cars in front of it
• 	If you can show your prospects that they’re not alone people will
want it
• 	Scarcity
• 	When there is less of something people will inherently want it more
• 	Event-based launches naturally lend themselves to elements of
scarcity
• 	Authority
• 	People follow authority figures
• 	Just by doing a prelaunch you can establish massive authority
• 	Anticipation
• 	When there’s anticipation it really captures our attention and
imagination
• 	This is how people get pulled into your launch
• 	Common enemy
!
2	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 2 of 5 --

• 	It’s shocking to see what people will do when there is a common
enemy - you can use this to your advantage
• 	Proof
• 	Show people that your stuff works - testimonials
• 	Controversy
• 	It gets people’s attention
• 	Commitment and consistency
• 	People like to act consistently with how they’ve acted in the past
• 	Interaction/conversation
• 	People would rather talk than listen
• 	People will pay attention to a conversation longer than a lecture
• 	Reciprocity
• 	Give great stuff away and people will naturally want to give back to
you
• 	Surprise/unexpectedness
• 	You don’t want to be predictable
• 	Likability
• 	People buy from those they know, like, and trust
!
3	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 3 of 5 --

• 	Be as likable in your launch as possible
• 	Credibility
• 	During a launch you have opportunities to establish that you’re
credible
• 	Celebrity/Being interesting
• 	Be seen doing unusual things
• 	Being seen as different
• 	This is easier than you’d expect - get creative
• 	Reason/Why
• 	“Because is a very powerful word”
• 	Even if your reason isn’t great this is a solid strategy
• 	Competition
• 	If you can get people competing for something it’s a great trigger
• 	Take-away-sale
• 	You sell from a powerful position of “take it or leave it”
• 	Naturally very attractive - if you act like you don’t need it/them, they’ll
pursue it more
• 	Simplicity
!
4	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 4 of 5 --

• 	Keep your messaging simple - it’s more powerful this way
• 	Specificity/concreteness
• 	The more exact you can be the better
• 	Ex: Ivory soap is 99.25% pure, not 99%
• 	Emotion
• 	If you can get their emotions involved it’s easier to influence them
!
5	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.

-- 5 of 5 --
